reasoning: Look, I'm not celebrating anything here, but the choice is clear. The ICP expansion happened under central government money — I see it every day, better lanes, new building. That's real delivery. Biswajit Das for TMC? A man who switches sides whenever it suits him? That's exactly the kind of game I've been saying TMC plays. The union gundas still come to my depot asking for their cut, yes, but at least Modi's government builds roads and ports. The ED raids, the central pressure — they're imperfect, but they're something. TMC just takes its cuts and builds nothing for people like me. I voted BJP in 2019 for infrastructure and to push back on the corruption machine, and I'm voting for the same reason today.
primary_drivers: Infrastructure delivery (ICP expansion is tangible proof)
Anti-TMC corruption stance (Biswajit Das defection proves opportunism)
Pragmatic belief that central action is imperfect but better than local rot
